rows.Title: Transition Portfolio Manager, Vice President, State Street Investment Management
rows.Full Description:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking candidates with 10+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics.

This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office.

Why this role is important to us

State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter

This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As a Transition Portfolio Manager, you will:

Utilize your in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle (pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects) to lead portfolio restructures for KSA-domiciled clients including complex multi-manager, multi-asset class events.Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions).Cover trade reconciliation process between internal systems for accuracy and completeness as needed prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades.Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert on multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies.Apply your expertise on benchmarking and measurement of transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA)Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topicsAssess internal business operations (trading strategies, performance, technology, data management, marketing, research) and develop solutions to enhance product positioningLead and contribute to strategic projects in partnership with other groups / businesses within State Street as well as external vendorsAssist in evolving and industrializing our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors


What We Value

The following skills will help you succeed in this role:

Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classesFamiliarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / toolsClient-facing experience leading complex transitions or portfolio implementations; strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsTechnical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposesSelf-starter with ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on detailsStrong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team


Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar10+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational roleMBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or CFA designation preferred


Additional Requirements

CMA registration requiredWillingness to travel up to 10% of the timeExperience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus


About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to

rows.Title: Chief Financial Officer
rows.Full Description: Purpose The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) is responsible for the overall financial stewardship of Ooredoo Fintech, ensuring robust financial governance, regulatory compliance, and operational discipline across the organization, in a fast growing, multi country environment. The role supports the company’s continued growth and operational scale by maintaining strong financial controls, reliable reporting, and efficient financial operations. In addition, the CFO will play a key role in driving transformation within the finance function, strengthening processes, systems, and capabilities to support a maturing fintech platform and an evolving regulatory environment. This is a hands-on role suited to a leader with a builder mentality, highly structured, with a strong personal presence and gravitas. Context OFT is an innovative and dynamic scale-up fintech company. We offer the heritage and stability of a hugely successful founding group combined with the unlimited ambition and passion of a talented team building intuitive, secure and convenient money management and payment tools for everyday people. The company operates digital wallet and payment services, including remittances, card transactions, airtime top-ups, and payroll flows. Maintaining customer trust, regulatory licence adherence, and financial resilience is critical. The business currently operates in 4 countries (Qatar, Oman, Tunisia and Maldives) and it is building in 3 additional countries (Iraq, Algeria, Kuwait). The business is the most ambitious fintech business sin the MENA region and is run with a highly centralised operating model, and high reusability of centrally defined process, systems and procedures. Working with us presents a unique and exciting opportunity to make a difference and be part of a revolution where we are leveraging financial technology to provide accessible, empowering tools to consumer and merchants. Role Accountabilities 1. Financial Strategy & Business Partnership Define and lead the financial strategy of the business, ensuring full alignment with corporate objectives, growth ambitions, and regulatory expectations. Act as a trusted strategic advisor to the CEO and Executive Committee, providing forward-looking financial insight to support product innovation, market expansion, and capital deployment decisions Drive long-term financial planning, including capital allocation, funding strategy, and value creation across markets and product lines. 2. Financial Governance, Control & Reporting Establish and maintain a best-in-class financial control environment, ensuring full compliance with IFRS and all applicable local regulatory requirements. Oversee the timely and accurate delivery of management accounts, board reporting, statutory financial statements, and regulatory submissions Ensure audit readiness at all times, with robust governance frameworks, policies, and controls embedded across the organisation. Own the integrity, consistency, and transparency of all financial data and reporting. 3. Finance Transformation, Systems & Scalability Lead the transformation of the finance function into a scalable, technology-enabled, and data-driven organisation. Design and implement robust, end-to-end processes and control frameworks that support growth, multi-country operations, and increasing regulatory complexity. Champion automation, system optimisation, and advanced analytics to improve efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making capability. Partner closely with Product and Technology teams to ensure financial architecture, reconciliation tools, and data flows are fit for scale and future innovation. 4. Financial Operations & Liquidity Management Provide executive oversight of all financial operations, including reconciliations, settlement processes, escrow management, and partner funding flows. Ensure robust safeguarding of customer funds, strict adherence to settlement SLAs, and effective fund segregation practices Lead liquidity management, including cash forecasting, working capital optimisation, and foreign exchange exposure management. Maintain operational resilience across all financial processes, ensuring continuity under stress scenarios. 5. Regulatory Engagement & Compliance Serve as the primary finance interface with regulators, central banks, auditors, and external stakeholders. Ensure full compliance with all licensing conditions, regulatory reporting requirements, and fintech/payment services regulations. Oversee financial aspects of AML/FCC frameworks in collaboration with Risk and Compliance functions. Proactively manage regulatory relationships, anticipating changes and ensuring the organisation remains ahead of evolving requirements. 6. Performance Management & Value Creation Lead all FP&A activities, including budgeting, forecasting, and performance tracking across markets and products. Translate complex financial and transactional data into clear, actionable insights for senior management and the Board. Drive dis

rows.Title: Head of Cabin Crew Training
rows.Location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
rows.Full Description: Synopsis The Head of Cabin Crew Training is a senior leadership role responsible for shaping and leading the delivery of Cabin Crew training across the airline. The role ensures the airline’s service ambition, brand promise, and premium proposition are consistently translated into high‑quality customer experiences. This position provides strategic leadership across safety & service training delivery while actively contributing to the evolution of the cabin crew training strategy. Working closely with training design, innovation, inflight service, and operational stakeholders, the role ensures Safety & Service training remains aligned with premium standards, evolving customer expectations, and future business needs. With a strong focus on service elevation, premium excellence, and innovation, the Head of Cabin Crew Training ensures training delivery is scalable, efficient, and capable of sustaining world‑class service standards at pace. Accountabilities Shape and contribute to the cabin crew training strategy, ensuring training priorities align with brand positioning, premium service ambition, and long‑term customer experience objectives. Lead and govern the delivery of cabin crew training across initial, recurrent, and enhancement programmes, ensuring consistent execution of approved strategies. Translate service strategy into effective training delivery, embedding required competence, behaviours, standards, and service rituals through high‑quality learning experiences. Partner with Training Design, Innovation, and Service stakeholders to shape and influence the development and evolution of training frameworks, curricula, and methodologies. Drive continuous service elevation through training, using delivery insights, operational feedback, and customer data to inform improvements in training approach and content. Champion innovation in training delivery, shaping the adoption of new tools, technologies, and experiential learning methods to enhance engagement, realism, and efficiency. Ensure training delivery models support scale and efficiency, balancing premium service outcomes with optimal use of instructors, facilities, and training time. Set clear expectations for instructor capability and role‑model behaviour, ensuring trainers consistently demonstrate and reinforce premium standards. Provide strategic oversight of service training performance, identifying risks, trends, and opportunities that inform future training strategy and investment decisions. Represent cabin crew training at senior governance forums, contributing insight on service capability, readiness, and training‑led service improvement. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree level education. Minimum of 12 years’ experience within the airline operation (aviation) industry, including at least 5 years in a senior management role leading team. Extensive airline cabin crew experience is strongly preferred, with a solid foundation in inflight service delivery, premium service standards, and customer experience execution About Etihad Airways Etihad Airways, the national airline of the UAE, was formed in 2003 and quickly went on to become one of the world’s leading airlines. From its home in Abu Dhabi, Etihad flies to passenger and cargo destinations in the Middle East, Africa, Europe, Asia, Australia and North America. Together with Etihad’s codeshare partners, Etihad’s network offers access to hundreds of international destinations. In recent years, Etihad has received numerous awards for its superior service and products, cargo offering, loyalty programme and more. All this ties into Etihad’s ambitious Journey 2030 strategy. The airline plans to double its fleet size and triple the number of customers over the next six years as it sets out to be the airline everyone wants to fly! To learn more, visit etihad.com Recruitment Fraud Alert Beware of fraudulent job offers from individuals or organizations claiming to represent the Etihad group. We will never ask for personal information, bank details, or payment during the recruitment process. Interviews are conducted face-to-face or via video/telephone before any formal offer. If you are asked for money, please treat it as fraudulent.

rows.Title: Fund Services.Vice President
rows.Location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
rows.Full Description: Job Description Client Integration VP About The Role The role of a Client Integration Vice President (VP) involves leading/overseeing client integrations, conversions and strategic projects. The Integration VP is responsible for the management of virtual project teams tasked with project managing client integrations/conversions, services delivery changes and sales support. The VP is responsible for managing a group of Assistant Vice Presidents (AVP), training and development of staff, role out of global best practice and standardization across the global Integration team. The core skills required are having experience in leading teams for successful delivery of projects within a project management framework, demonstrating effective communication skills, having client-facing experience and understanding the financial services business. The VP will be heavily involved in securing new business as well as manage existing client relationships and onboardings, will have a lead role in global or local initiatives interacting with other Citco global offices and technology groups The integration team is the central point of contact for the client both technical and support during onboarding. The role involves dealing with the senior management of all the organizations that interact with Citco Fund Services. Responsibilities Sales Preparation: Participate in marketing and sales pitches; effectively demonstrate and communicate Citco’s workflows, best practices and technologies Request for proposal/information assistance Direct source of information to Business Development Stakeholder in the development of new clients/business AA / SLA negotiation Manage requested Proof of Concepts for sales pitches Project Manage External New client launches/conversions New Business Existing Client (NBEC) launches Onboarding of Ancillary services Coordinate with all relevant teams to ensure internal workflows will meet client needs and they capture any unique sets of requirements. Coordinate with both internal and external stakeholders to ensure the project scope is documented, agreed and completed at planning phases. Major SLA changes Project Manage Internal Actively participate in departmental and company-wide projects. Process Improvements Oversee and manage strategic initiatives within the Client Integration team Identify issues/concerns and recommend company-wide solutions for any/all high risk projects Administrative Reporting Tracking of projects, progress, status and deadlines MIS Reporting Actively manage operational risk – monthly reporting on status reports and key development issues to external clients and executive management team. Ensure strong emphasis on escalation and complaints management within the team and within the department. Ensure fluid execution and strategy for work forecasting and allocation Management In partnership with the Senior Vice Presidents, oversee the resolution of complex client issues. Management of virtual teams Pipeline/Project/Resourcing Management Oversight, Recruitment, Training and Development. Review departmental procedures and policies and help ensure adherence to these procedures Qualifications Degree qualified or related professional qualification Experiences 5+ years of project management experience 3+ years of client services within the asset management industry 3+ years of people and resource management experience 7+ years in the financial services industry Proven interest in the financial markets and hedge fund industry Proven Project Management Experience Proven Business Analysis Experience Proven knowledge of Listed and OTC products Proficient in Microsoft Office applications Ability to handle several projects and different stakeholders simultaneously, and prioritize work based on deadlines A ‘team player’ who can work well within the function to deliver a quality service Strong sense of ownership and accountability of work product Creative and innovative Continuous improvement Desirable Accounting experience (Desirable) Middle Office experience (Desirable) Project Manager Qualifications (Desirable) Relationship Management Experience (Desirable)
